passages.title: When the Tulip Bubble Burst
passages.source_html:
When the Tulip Bubble Burst 


Tulips are spring-blooming perennials that grow from bulbs. Depending on the species, tulip plants can grow as short as 4 inches (10 cm) or as high as 28 inches (71 cm). The tulip’s large flowers usually bloom on scapes or sub-scapose stems that lack bracts. Most tulips produce only one flower per stem, but a few species bear multiple flowers on their scapes (e.g. Tulipa turkestanica). The showy, generally cup or star-shaped tulip flower has three petals and three sepals, which are often termed tepals because they are nearly identical. These six tepals are often marked on the interior surface near the bases with darker colorings. Tulip flowers come in a wide variety of colors, except pure blue (several tulips with ‘blue’ in the name have a faint violet hue).


A 


Long before anyone ever heard of Qualcomm, CMGI, Cisco Systems, or the other high-tech stocks that have soared during the current bull market, there was Semper Augustus. Both more prosaic and more subline than any stock or bond, it was a tulip of extraordinary beauty, its midnight-blue petals topped by a band of pure white and accented with crimson flares. To denizens of 17th century Holland, little was as desirable. 


B 


Around 1624, the Amsterdam man who owned the only dozen specimens was offered 3,000 guilders for one bulb. While there’s no accurate way to render that in today’s greenbacks, the sum was roughly equal to the annual income of a wealthy merchant. A few years later, Rembrandt received about half that amount for painting The Night Watch. Yet the bulb’s owner, whose name is now lost to history, nixed the offer. 


C 


Who was crazier, the tulip lover who refused to sell for a small fortune or the one who was willing to splurge. That’s a question that springs to mind after reading Tulipmania: The Story of the World’s Most Coveted Flower and the Extraordinary Passions It Aroused by British journalist Mike Dash. In recent years, as investors have intentionally forgotten everything they learned in Investing 101 in order to load up on unproven, unprofitable dot-com issues, tulip mania has been invoked frequently. In this concise, artfully written account, Dash tells the real history behind the buzzword and in doing so, offers a cautionary late for our times. 


D 


The Dutch were not the first to go gaga over the tulip. Long before the first tulip bloomed in Europe – in Bavaria, it turns out, in 1559 – the flower had enchanted the Persians and bewitched the rulers of the Ottoman Empire. It was in Holland, however, that the passion for tulips found its most fertile ground, for reasons that had little to do with horticulture. 


E 


Holland in the early 17th century was embarking on its Golden Age. Resources that had just a few years earlier gone toward fighting for independence from Spain now flowed into commerce. Amsterdam merchants were at the center of the lucrative East Indies trade, where a single voyage could yield profits of 400%. They displayed their success by erecting grand estates surrounded by flower gardens. The Dutch population seemed torn by two contradictory impulses: a horror of living beyond one’s means and the love of a long shot. 


F 


Enter the tulip. ‘It is impossible to comprehend the tulip mania without understanding just how different tulips were from every other flower known to horticulturists in the 17th century,’ says Dash, ‘The colors they exhibited were more intense and more concentrated than those of ordinary plants.’ Despite the outlandish prices commanded by rare bulbs, ordinary tulips were sold by the pound. Around 1630, however, a new type of tulip fancier appeared, lured by tales of fat profits. These ‘florists’, or professional tulip traders, sought out flower lovers and speculators alike. But if the supply of tulip buyers grew quickly, the supply of bulbs did not. The tulip was a conspirator in the supply squeeze: It takes seven years to grow one from seed. And while bulbs can produce two or three clones, or ‘offsets’, annually, the mother bulb only lasts a few years.


G 


Bulb prices rose steadily throughout the 1630s, as ever more speculators wedged into the market. Weavers and farmers mortgaged whatever they could to raise cash to begin trading. In 1633, a farmhouse in Hoorn changed hands for three rare bulbs. By 1636 any tulip-even bulbs recently considered garbage – could be sold off, often for hundreds of guilders. A futures market for bulbs existed, and tulip traders could be found conducting their business in hundreds of Dutch taverns. Tulipmania reached its peak during the winter of 1636-37 when some bulbs were changing hands ten times in a day. The zenith came early that winter, at an auction to benefit seven orphans whose only asset was 70 fine tulips left by their father. One, a rare Violetten Admirael van Enkhuizen bulb that was about to split in two, sold for 5,200 guilders, the all-time record. All told, the flowers brought in nearly 53,000 guilders. 


H 


Soon after, the tulip market crashed utterly, spectacularly. It began in Haarlem, at a routine bulb auction when, for the first time, the greater fool refused to show up and pay. Within days, the panic had spread across the country. Despite the efforts of traders to prop up demand, the market for tulips evaporated. Flowers that had commanded 5,000 guilders a few weeks before now fetched one-hundredth that amount. Tulipmania is not without flaws. Dash dwells too long on the tulip’s migration from Asia to Holland. But he does a service with this illuminating, accessible account of incredible financial folly. 


I 


Tulipmania differed in one crucial aspect from the dot-com craze that grips our attention today: even at its height, the Amsterdam Stock Exchange, well-established in 1630, wouldn’t touch tulips. ‘The speculation in tulip bulbs always existed at the margins of Dutch economic life,’ Dash writes. After the market crashed, a compromise was brokered that let most traders settle their debts for a fraction of their liability. The overall fallout on the Dutch economy was negligible. Will we say the same when Wall Street’s current obsession finally runs its course?

groups.title: Questions 14-27
groups.source_html:
Questions 14-18


The reading passage has 9 paragraphs A-I. Which paragraph contains the following information? Write the correct letter A-l, in boxes 14-18 on your answer sheet.


Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
14

14 Difference between bubble burst impacts by tulip and by high-tech shares.
✅ 正确答案：I

原文答案句：Tulipmania differed in one crucial aspect from the dot-com craze that grips our attention today: even at its height, the Amsterdam Stock Exchange, well-established in 1630, wouldn’t touch tulips.  

对应段落：I段  

审题：题目问的是“郁金香泡沫破裂与高科技股泡沫破裂的影响差异”这一信息出现在哪一段。  

定位关键信息：题目中的核心词汇是“bubble burst impacts by tulip and by high-tech shares”，即“郁金香泡沫破裂与高科技股泡沫破裂的影响差异”。通过“bubble burst”和“high-tech shares”可以定位到文章中对比两类泡沫影响的句子。  

解题思路：在I段中，原文明确提到“Tulipmania differed in one crucial aspect from the dot-com craze”，即郁金香狂热与互联网泡沫在关键方面存在差异，随后具体解释了这种差异在于阿姆斯特丹证券交易所不参与郁金香交易，而高科技股泡沫则与之不同。这一内容直接对应题目所问的“两类泡沫破裂影响的差异”，因此答案为I段。
15

15 Spread of tulip before 17th century.
✅ 正确答案：D

原文答案句：Long before the first tulip bloomed in Europe – in Bavaria, it turns out, in 1559 – the flower had enchanted the Persians and bewitched the rulers of the Ottoman Empire.

对应段落：D段

审题：题目问的是关于郁金香在17世纪之前的传播情况。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的"before 17th century"和"Spread of tulip"来定位，在D段中找到了"Long before the first tulip bloomed in Europe"和"the flower had enchanted the Persians and bewitched the rulers of the Ottoman Empire"，这两句话明确说明了郁金香在17世纪之前就已经传播到了欧洲以外的地区。

解题思路：根据原文中的句子，可以确定D段讲述了郁金香在17世纪之前的传播情况，包括在波斯和奥斯曼帝国的流行，以及在1559年首次在欧洲巴伐利亚绽放。因此，D段包含了题目所问的信息。
16

16 Indication of money offered for the rare bulb in the 17th century.
✅ 正确答案：B

原文答案句：Around 1624, the Amsterdam man who owned the only dozen specimens was offered 3,000 guilders for one bulb.  

对应段落：B段  

审题：题目问的是哪个段落提到了17世纪曾有人为稀有的郁金香球茎开出高价。  

定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词"money offered for the rare bulb in the 17th century"定位，在B段第一句中找到了"was offered 3,000 guilders for one bulb"（提供3000基尔德购买一个球茎），明确提到了17世纪为稀有球茎提供的金钱数额。  

解题思路：B段第一句明确提到了1624年（17世纪），一位阿姆斯特丹的男子拥有的稀有球茎，有人要以3000基尔德购买，这与题目中"money offered for the rare bulb in the 17th century"完全吻合，因此答案为B。
17

17 Tulip was treated as money in Holland.
✅ 正确答案：G

原文答案句：In 1633, a farmhouse in Hoorn changed hands for three rare bulbs.

对应段落：G段

审题：题目问“郁金香在荷兰被当作货币对待”，即郁金香具有货币的交换功能。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词"Holland"和"money"（货币）定位，在文章中寻找郁金香被当作交换媒介的描述。

解题思路：原文G段提到"1633年，在霍恩，一座农舍以三颗稀有郁金香球茎作为交换而转手"，这表明当时人们直接用郁金香球茎作为购买农舍的支付手段，相当于用郁金香作为货币进行交易。因此G段包含郁金香被当作货币对待的信息。
18

18 The comparison made between a tulip and other plants.
✅ 正确答案：F

原文答案句：‘It is impossible to comprehend the tulip mania without understanding just how different tulips were from every other flower known to horticulturists in the 17th century,’ says Dash, ‘The colors they exhibited were more intense and more concentrated than those of ordinary plants.’

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问的是哪一段落包含了将郁金香与其他植物进行比较的信息。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"comparison"和"tulip and other plants"可以定位到F段中描述郁金香颜色与其他植物进行比较的句子。

解题思路：F段中明确提到了郁金香的颜色比其他植物更加鲜艳和浓烈，这是将郁金香与其他植物进行比较的具体描述，因此F段包含了题目所要求的比较信息。

Questions 19-23


Do the following statements agree with the information given in passage? In boxes 19-23 on your answer sheet, write TURE if the statement is true, FALSE if the statement is false, NOT GIVEN if the information is not given in the passage.

 


19

In 1624, all the tulip collection belonged to a man in Amsterdam.

正确答案： A. TRUE

原文答案句：Around 1624, the Amsterdam man who owned the only dozen specimens was offered 3,000 guilders for one bulb.

对应段落：B段  

审题： 题目判断“在1624年，所有的郁金香藏品都属于一个阿姆斯特丹人”这一说法是否与文章信息一致。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词“1624”和“Amsterdam man”定位到B段第一句。

解题思路：原文明确指出，大约在1624年，“那个拥有唯一一批（共12株）样本的阿姆斯特丹人”收到了对一个球茎的报价。这里的“the only dozen specimens”（唯一的一批样本）说明，在当时，这种特定的、极其珍贵的郁金香的所有已知个体（即所有藏品）都归此人所有。这与题目中“all the tulip collection belonged to a man”的陈述相符。因此，该陈述为TRUE。


20

Tulip was first planted in Holland according to this passage.

正确答案： B. FALSE

原文答案句：Long before the first tulip bloomed in Europe – in Bavaria, it turns out, in 1559 – the flower had enchanted the Persians and bewitched the rulers of the Ottoman Empire. 

对应段落：D段  

审题：题目问“根据这篇文章，郁金香首先种植在荷兰”，需要判断这一说法是否正确。  

定位关键信息：题目中的“first planted in Holland”是关键词，在文章中定位到D段提到“the passion for tulips found its most fertile ground in Holland”，但未明确说明“first planted”。  

解题思路：原文D段提到在1559年，巴伐利亚，第一朵郁金香出现，后来在波斯和奥斯曼帝国备受喜爱，但并未明确说明郁金香“首先”种植在荷兰。因此，题目中的“first planted”与原文信息不符，应选择False。


21

The popularity of Tulip in Holland was much higher than any other countries in the 17th century.

正确答案： A. TRUE

原文答案句：It was in Holland, however, that the passion for tulips found its most fertile ground, for reasons that had little to do with horticulture.  

对应段落：D段  

审题：题目问“在17世纪，荷兰郁金香的受欢迎程度是否比任何其他国家都高”，这一说法是否与原文相符。  

定位关键信息：利用题目的关键词“Holland”、“17th century”、“popularity higher than any other countries”定位到D段，该段提到荷兰是郁金香热情最肥沃的土壤。  

解题思路：原文明确指出荷兰是郁金香最受欢迎的地方，虽然没有直接与其他国家比较“更高”，但“most fertile ground”暗示其受欢迎程度达到顶峰，可以理解为比其他国家更高。再者，根据上下文可知，郁金香狂热是发生在17世纪的荷兰，因此，题干与原文信息一致，答案为True。


22

Holland was the most wealthy country in the world in the 17th century.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

原文答案句：Holland in the early 17th century was embarking on its Golden Age. Resources that had just a few years earlier gone toward fighting for independence from Spain now flowed into commerce. Amsterdam merchants were at the center of the lucrative East Indies trade, where a single voyage could yield profits of 400%. They displayed their success by erecting grand estates surrounded by flower gardens.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目问的是“荷兰在17世纪是否是世界上最富裕的国家”。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的“Holland”、“17th century”、“most wealthy country in the world”等关键词定位答案在文章中的句子。

解题思路：原文确实描述了17世纪初荷兰进入“黄金时代”，商业繁荣，来自东印度贸易的利润丰厚，商人建造豪宅显示其成功。这些信息都表明荷兰在当时非常富有和繁荣。然而，原文只描述了荷兰自身的繁荣，并未将其财富与其他国家进行比较。因此，题目中的信息在原文中没有明确给出，答案应为“NOT GIVEN”。


23

From 1630, Amsterdam Stock Exchange started to regulate Tulips exchange market.

正确答案： B. FALSE

原文答案句：Tulipmania differed in one crucial aspect from the dot-com craze that grips our attention today: even at its height, the Amsterdam Stock Exchange, well-established in 1630, wouldn’t touch tulips. 

对应段落：I段  

审题：题目问“从1630年开始，阿姆斯特丹证券交易所开始监管郁金香交易市场”是否正确。  

定位关键信息：利用题目的关键词"1630"、"Amsterdam Stock Exchange"和"regulate Tulips exchange market"定位到I段首句。  

解题思路：原文提到阿姆斯特丹证券交易所早在1630年就已建立，但明确表示它"wouldn’t touch tulips"（不会参与郁金香交易），而题目说它开始监管郁金香市场，这与原文完全相反。因此，题目陈述是错误的，答案为False。


Questions 24-27

Complete the summary below. Using NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer. Write your answers in boxes 24-27 on your answer sheet.


24

Dutch concentrated on gaining independence by 24 against Spain in the early 17th century;

正确答案： 24 fighting##Fighting
Question 24

原文答案句：Resources that had just a few years earlier gone toward fighting for independence from Spain now flowed into commerce.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目问的是在17世纪初，荷兰通过什么方式对抗西班牙以获得独立。

定位关键信息：题目中的关键词是"17th century"、"independence from Spain"和"fighting"，通过这些关键词可以定位到E段中提到荷兰为了独立对抗西班牙的句子。

解题思路：原文中提到荷兰在早期为了对抗西班牙独立，花费了资源进行战斗。因此，答案为"fighting"。


25

consequently, spare resources entered the area of 25 .

正确答案： 25 commerce##Commerce
Question 25

原文答案句：Resources that had just a few years earlier gone toward fighting for independence from Spain now flowed into commerce.  

对应段落：E段  

审题：题目问的是“因此，剩余资源进入了哪个领域”。  

定位关键信息：利用题目的关键词"spare resources"（剩余资源）和"entered the area of"（进入了...领域）来定位原文。在E段中找到"Resources...now flowed into commerce."，这里的"Resources"对应"spare resources"，"flowed into"对应"entered"，"commerce"对应"the area of"。  

解题思路：原文明确提到资源从战争转向了商业，因此答案为"commerce"。


26

Prosperous traders demonstrated their status by building great 26 and with gardens in surroundings.

正确答案： 26 estates##Estates
Question 26

原文答案句：They displayed their success by erecting grand estates surrounded by flower gardens.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目问的是富裕的商人通过建造什么来展示他们的地位，并且这些建筑周围有花园。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词"Prosperous traders"、"demonstrated their status"、"building great"和"gardens in surroundings"来定位答案。

解题思路：根据原文句子"They displayed their success by erecting grand estates surrounded by flower gardens."，可以确定富裕的商人通过建造"grand estates"（宏伟的庄园）来展示他们的地位，并且这些庄园周围有花园。因此，答案为"estates"。


27

Attracted by the success of profit on tulip, traders kept looking for 27 and speculator for sale.

正确答案： 27 flower lovers##Flower Lovers
Question 27

原文答案句：These ‘florists’, or professional tulip traders, sought out flower lovers and speculators alike.  

对应段落：F段  

审题：题目问的是受到郁金香交易成功与利润的吸引，商人们不断寻找有意购买的什么和投机者。  

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"traders"和"speculator"定位到F段，找到描述交易者寻找对象的句子。  

解题思路：根据原文F段中的句子"professional tulip traders, sought out flower lovers and speculators alike"，可以确定交易者寻找的对象是"flower lovers"，即花爱好者。题目中的"27"对应的是"flower lovers"这一群体。
